---
title: "crop_to_multiple"
generated: true
description: "Crop the leading `spatial_rank` axes down to a multiple."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.numerics.spatial`](../nitrix-numerics-spatial.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:crop_to_multiple -->
```python
crop_to_multiple(x: Num[Array, '...'], multiple: _IntArg, *, spatial_rank: int) -> Tuple[Num[Array, '...'], Tuple[Tuple[int, int], ...]]
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:crop_to_multiple -->
Crop the leading `spatial_rank` axes down to a multiple.

Each spatial axis is symmetrically trimmed to the largest multiple of
`multiple` not exceeding its length. When the excess is odd, the
extra element is removed from the high side of the axis. Trailing
axes (e.g. channels) are left untouched.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:crop_to_multiple -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `x` | `Num` | `...` | — | Input array whose first `spatial_rank` axes are spatial. |
| `multiple` | `_IntArg` | — | — | Target multiple for each spatial axis. A single integer is broadcast to every spatial axis; a sequence must have length `spatial_rank`. |
| `spatial_rank` | `int` | — | — | Number of leading axes treated as spatial. |

**Returns** `Tuple[Num[Array, '...'], Tuple[Tuple[int, int], ...]]`. The cropped array. The per-spatial-axis `(lo, hi)` amounts removed from each spatial axis.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:crop_to_multiple -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
